text: Hi, I'm Dr. Lillian Shapiro, a robotic surgeon and gynecologist practicing in Atlanta, Georgia. I would like to show you clips from a robotic hysterectomy. A robotic hysterectomy involves the same steps as a traditional hysterectomy, but is done with instruments through tiny incisions.

The surgeon locates the round ligament, a support structure with visible blood vessels, and transects it. Subsequently, the bladder is carefully separated from the uterus, creating a bladder flap. The uterus appears significantly enlarged compared to a normal size.
The surgeon uses robotic instruments to remove the fallopian tubes, which are visibly detached from the surrounding tissue. The ovaries are left intact to prevent hormonal changes.

The surgeon disconnects the ovary from the uterus by severing the utero-ovarian ligament. The ovary remains attached to the pelvis via the infundibulopelvic ligament, maintaining its main blood supply. The thick uterine artery, which supplies blood to the uterus, is visible and controlled, preparing for the disconnection of the uterus.

A firm cap is placed at the cervico-vaginal junction to guide the dissection during the abdominal portion of the surgery. The surgeon delicately sutures the vaginal cuff, completing this part of the hysterectomy procedure.
I would be happy to discuss your options with you. Send me an email at ideogynecology at gmail.com.
